name: Typeform API
what_it_does: Conversational form and survey platform API. Allows creating and managing forms, retrieving responses, and triggering webhooks on submission. Known for high-completion-rate conversational UX.
use_cases: Creating dynamic forms and surveys programmatically for data collection
Retrieving form responses for processing in AI agent workflows
Triggering agent actions when a form is submitted via webhooks
Building qualification flows and lead capture with conditional logic
Automating form creation for event registrations and feedback collection
not_for: High-volume data collection where cost per response matters
Complex multi-page surveys with advanced branching logic at scale
best_when: An agent needs to trigger workflows when forms are submitted, or needs to programmatically create conversational forms with high completion rates.
avoid_when: You need very complex survey logic, offline forms, or cost-effective high-volume data collection.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Personal access tokens available for personal use and server-side agents. Token passed as Bearer in Authorization header. Scopes control access to forms, responses, webhooks.

pricing.paid_tiers: Basic $29/month (unlimited questions, 100 responses), Plus $59/month (1000 responses), Business $99/month (10K responses)
pricing.notes: Free tier is quite restrictive on responses. Per-response model can get expensive at scale. Annual plans offer significant discount. Response limits are the main constraint for agents.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned as JSON with code and description. Standard HTTP status codes used. Error messages are generally clear. Rate limit errors include X-Rate-Limit headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Form creation is not idempotent. Agents should check for existing forms before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Response limits on all plans — agents must monitor usage to avoid hitting caps mid-campaign
Form schema can be complex with many block types — agents need to understand Typeform's field model
Webhook payload validation requires verifying Typeform-Signature header
Conditional logic (Logic Jumps) has a complex configuration structure
Hidden fields (prefilled data) must be set in the form embed URL, not via API response
File upload responses return temporary URLs that expire — agents must download promptly
